title: Monthly Owner Report
description: Build a monthly owner report for a commercial asset covering occupancy, arrears, operating performance, incidents, works, and decisions needed.
whyItMatters: Owner reporting is where a property manager's judgment becomes visible. Great reports reduce friction, sharpen decision-making, and build trust because they tell the story behind the numbers instead of dumping raw detail.
skillsLearned: Explaining operational and financial variance clearly
Connecting service issues to asset performance
Framing decisions and approvals for owners

steps.title: Assemble the operating facts
steps.description: Create a property snapshot with occupancy, arrears, service issues, notable tenant activity, spend versus budget, major work orders, and risk watch-list items.
steps.tips: Do not start by writing prose. Start by collecting the facts you would be comfortable defending in a client meeting.

steps.title: Choose the story
steps.description: Use the Owner Report Storyline to identify the key messages for the month. Decide what changed, why it changed, and what matters most to asset value or tenant confidence.
steps.tips: If every issue gets the same weight, the report becomes background noise.

steps.title: Write the narrative and decision asks
steps.description: Draft the report with headline summary, KPI table, commentary, works update, tenant and leasing notes, and any decisions or approvals required.
steps.tips: Be direct about what you need from the owner: approval, awareness, strategy input, or nothing.

steps.title: Pressure-test the report
steps.description: Review the report as if you were the owner. Remove jargon, sharpen the variance commentary, and confirm every recommendation is evidence-backed.
steps.tips: A good test question is: 'Could this be read aloud in a 10-minute client call without confusion?'

deliverables: A monthly owner report deck or memo
A KPI table covering occupancy, collections, spend, and service performance
Three to five concise commentary sections explaining the month
A clear approval or decision list for the owner

rubric.description: The report has a clear point of view and separates the important story from routine detail.
Commentary connects operations, finance, tenant issues, and risk in a commercially sensible way.
Metrics are relevant, easy to scan, and tied to the asset's actual priorities.
The report makes it obvious what the owner should approve, note, or challenge next.
